heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 225, which occurred on 28 May 2026 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: Currently no stormy weather.  The most recent storm happened on 18 July 2026 and it lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 03 July 2026, is ranked 1st among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 21 days so far.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C), set on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: There is currently a heatwave.  It has been going on for 23 days and it started on 2026-07-01.  Currently this year there have been 3 heatwaves, usually there are 1.9 heatwaves per year.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 28 June 2026, making a total of only 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (218°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 27 January 2026 with a value of 128.2 mph.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 6218 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 9939 lux), cloud levels (up 766 m), and feels like temperature (up 1.7°C) when compared to today.

summary.summary: Inside; hot for most of the day. Outside; the temperature will feel quite hot. Also, a strong muggy feeling will dominate and very little to no wind will be present. Drizzle or light rain possible, the clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected, and bright sunshine will dominate the day. No significant shifts in the weather are expected.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
